---
title: "Mercado Laboral"
subtitle: "Empleo y desempleo en Chile"
date: last-modified
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as ticker

url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Economia-y-politicas-publicas/chiledatos/main/data/trabajo.csv"

dt = pd.read_csv(url, sep=",", encoding="utf-8")
dt = dt.rename(columns={
    "Tiempo": "periodo", "Año": "año", "Mes": "mes",
    "Total": "total", "Hombre": "hombre", "Mujer": "mujer"
})
dt[["año","mes","total","hombre","mujer"]] = dt[["año","mes","total","hombre","mujer"]].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce")
dt = dt.dropna(subset=["año","mes","total"])
dt = dt[(dt["año"] >= 2010) & (dt["año"] <= 2030)]  # filtro de seguridad
dt["fecha"] = pd.to_datetime(dict(year=dt["año"], month=dt["mes"], day=1))
dt = dt.sort_values("fecha").reset_index(drop=True)
dt["brecha"] = dt["mujer"] - dt["hombre"]

AUTOR = "Autor: Juan Miguieles Onetto | Fuente: INE"

plt.rcParams.update({
    "font.family": "sans-serif",
    "axes.spines.top": False,
    "axes.spines.right": False,
    "figure.dpi": 120,
})
```

## 1. Tasa de desempleo total

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Tasa de desempleo total en Chile (%)"

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(dt["fecha"], dt["total"], color="#f39c12", linewidth=2)
ax.set_title("Tasa de desempleo total en Chile (%)", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", pad=12)
ax.set_xlabel("Año")
ax.set_ylabel("Tasa de desempleo (%)")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FormatStrFormatter("%.1f"))
ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(ticker.MaxNLocator(10))
fig.autofmt_xdate(rotation=0, ha="center")
fig.text(0, -0.05, AUTOR, fontsize=9, color="gray", transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0, -0.08, AUTOR, fontsize=9, color="gray", transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0, -0.14, "Nota: La tasa de desempleo se mide trimestralmente mediante la ENE.", 
         fontsize=9, color="#aaaaaa", transform=ax.transAxes, style="italic")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## 2. Desempleo por sexo

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Tasa de desempleo por sexo (%)"

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(dt["fecha"], dt["total"],  color="#f39c12", linewidth=2, label="Total")
ax.plot(dt["fecha"], dt["hombre"], color="#2980b9", linewidth=2, label="Hombre")
ax.plot(dt["fecha"], dt["mujer"],  color="#e74c3c", linewidth=2, label="Mujer")
ax.set_title("Tasa de desempleo por sexo (%)", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", pad=12)
ax.set_xlabel("Año")
ax.set_ylabel("Tasa de desempleo (%)")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FormatStrFormatter("%.1f"))
ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(ticker.MaxNLocator(10))
ax.legend(loc="upper right", frameon=False)
fig.autofmt_xdate(rotation=0, ha="center")
fig.text(0, -0.05, AUTOR, fontsize=9, color="gray", transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0, -0.08, AUTOR, fontsize=9, color="gray", transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0, -0.14, "Nota: La tasa de desempleo se mide trimestralmente mediante la ENE.", 
         fontsize=9, color="#aaaaaa", transform=ax.transAxes, style="italic")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## 3. Brecha de desempleo mujer − hombre

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Brecha de desempleo mujer − hombre (puntos porcentuales)"

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.fill_between(dt["fecha"], dt["brecha"], color="#8e44ad", alpha=0.4)
ax.plot(dt["fecha"], dt["brecha"], color="#8e44ad", linewidth=1.5)
ax.axhline(0, color="gray", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8)
ax.set_title("Brecha de desempleo mujer − hombre (pp)", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", pad=12)
ax.set_xlabel("Año")
ax.set_ylabel("Puntos porcentuales")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FormatStrFormatter("%.1f"))
ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(ticker.MaxNLocator(10))
fig.autofmt_xdate(rotation=0, ha="center")
fig.text(0, -0.05, AUTOR, fontsize=9, color="gray", transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0, -0.08, AUTOR, fontsize=9, color="gray", transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0, -0.14, "Nota: La tasa de desempleo se mide trimestralmente mediante la ENE.", 
         fontsize=9, color="#aaaaaa", transform=ax.transAxes, style="italic")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
